---
title: "LRPHS01: Regularized-horseshoe ranking cross-check - word-reading gain"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPHS01 is an **independent Bayesian sensitivity check** on the gradient-boosting
predictor ranking for word-reading gain (GB `lrpgbg12`). A regularized (Finnish)
horseshoe sparse regression regresses the word-reading post-count on its own T1
baseline plus the standardised T1 baselines of the other constructs, all sharing a
global-local shrinkage prior; predictors are ranked by posterior
`P(|beta| > delta)` and compared to the GB *cluster* ranking at construct level.

_Ranking cross-check, NOT causal._ If the horseshoe order broadly agrees with the gradient-boosting order, that is
reassurance the ranking is not an artefact of the tree method — it is not a causal
claim. Every coefficient is a **latent-ability-confounded adjusted association**;
the randomised causal effect lives in the ITT suite (`lrp-rli-itt-010` for word reading).

_Between-child framing, n ~ 54._ One row per child (T1 baselines → full-study word-reading gain, `W_last | W_T1`),
mirroring LRP65: the coefficients are between-child associations. At n ~ 54 with a
shrinkage prior the intervals are wide by design; read the *ordering*, not the
magnitudes. Hyperparameters are weakly-informative priors, not tuned.

## Model

Beta-Binomial word-reading post-count conditioned on its own T1 baseline
(`gamma_own`), with standardised T1 predictor baselines entering under a
regularized-horseshoe prior (global scale `tau`, per-predictor local scale
`lambda_k`, slab `c^2`):

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(W_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
        + \sum_k \beta_k\, z_{k,i},
\qquad
\beta_k \sim \mathcal{N}\!\big(0,\ \tau\, \tilde{\lambda}_k\big).
$$

Construct predictor set: letter sounds `L`, receptive/expressive vocabulary
`R`/`E`, blending `B`, basic concepts `F`, receptive grammar `T`, age, and the
RLI-blocks / behaviour covariates. Floored / post-only measures (`P`, `N`) and the
block-1 taught/not-taught vocabulary (unconfirmed denominators) are omitted.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_horseshoe.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
